title: Conotruncal Defects: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Conotruncal Defects
Identify key diagnostic features of Conotruncal Defects
Apply clinical reasoning to Conotruncal Defects management
Recognize common pitfalls in Conotruncal Defects

sections.title: Overview of Conotruncal Defects
sections.content:
**Conotruncal Defects**

Neural crest cell migration, truncal septation, Tetralogy of Fallot, transposition, truncus arteriosus, 22q11 deletion

**Key Points:**
• Neural crest cells form the aorticopulmonary septum (divides truncus into aorta and pulmonary artery)
• Failed spiral septation → Transposition of Great Arteries (parallel circulations)
• Unequal septation → Tetralogy of Fallot (anterior deviation of infundibular septum)

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• Neural crest cells form the aorticopulmonary septum (divides truncus into aorta and pulmonary artery)
• Failed spiral septation → Transposition of Great Arteries (parallel circulations)

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• Unequal septation → Tetralogy of Fallot (anterior deviation of infundibular septum)
• No septation → Persistent Truncus Arteriosus
• 22q11.2 deletion (DiGeorge): conotruncal defects + absent thymus + hypocalcemia + facial dysmorphism

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for Conotruncal Defects:**

• Unequal septation → Tetralogy of Fallot (anterior deviation of infundibular septum)
• No septation → Persistent Truncus Arteriosus
• 22q11.2 deletion (DiGeorge): conotruncal defects + absent thymus + hypocalcemia + facial dysmorphism

mcqs.question: Which of the following is MOST characteristic of Conotruncal Defects?

options.text: Neural crest cells form the aorticopulmonary septum (divides truncus into aorta and pulmonary artery)
options.explanation: Neural crest cells form the aorticopulmonary septum (divides truncus into aorta and pulmonary artery)

options.text: Requires invasive testing in all cases

mcqs.question: What is the KEY diagnostic or management consideration for Conotruncal Defects?

options.text: 22q11.2 deletion (DiGeorge): conotruncal defects + absent thymus + hypocalcemia + facial dysmorphism
options.explanation: 22q11.2 deletion (DiGeorge): conotruncal defects + absent thymus + hypocalcemia + facial dysmorphism

options.text: Immediate surgical intervention in all cases

mcqs.question: A patient presents with findings consistent with Conotruncal Defects. Which finding would you expect?

options.text: Failed spiral septation → Transposition of Great Arteries (parallel circulations)
options.explanation: Failed spiral septation → Transposition of Great Arteries (parallel circulations)
